description: To ensure the drinking water for the largest portion of Michigan residents was tested for PFAS, beginning in April 2018, the Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE) implemented a statewide survey to test Michigan's public water supplies across several phases of sampling. *Results are provided in parts per trillion. A part per trillion (ppt) is the equivalent of 1 drop of water in 20 Olympic-size swimming pools FOR FULL DETAILS ABOUT THE DATASET, VISIT: https://www.mich

description: Between 2018 and early 2020 EGLE sampled PFAS foam at Van Etten Lake and Cedar Lake in Oscoda near the former Wurtsmith Air Force Base and on Lake Margrethe in Grayling near Camp Grayling. Foam was also seen and tested at several other locations. Note that there are no standards for PFAS in foam, so no regulatory actions can be taken due to these results. All results are shared with MDHHS so that they can evaluate the data to determine if a foam advisory is needed. For questions about this datas

description: This plant manual identifies plants and planting practices ideal for slope stabilization along urban highways. Appropriate plant selections are adapted to environmental stresses and harsh site conditions along depressed highway slopes found in urban areas. The plant selections also meet additional design criteria (e.g., low growing to allow clear vision, aesthetic appeal). This research was conducted by Michigan State University Department of Horticulture and Dr. Cregg. This research was funded

columns.description: The year the overlay was applied to the bridge deck.

columns.name: Year Painted - B.W.02
columns.description: The year the structure was last painted.

columns.name: Total Spans - Item 45+Item 46/B.SP.02
columns.description: The total number of spans on the structure.

columns.name: Length - Item 49/B.G.02
columns.description: The length of the structure measured along the centerline of the roadway from back-to-back of backwalls of abutments or from paving notch to paving notch to the nearest tenth of a foot.

columns.name: Bridge Width Out-to-Out - Item 52/B.G.05
columns.description: The out-to-out width of the deck to the nearest tenth of a foot. If the structure is a through structure, the number to be recorded will represent the lateral clearance between superstructure members. The measurement should be exclusive of flared areas for ramps. Where traffic runs directly on the top slab (or wearing surface) of the culvert, record the actual width (out-to-out). This will also apply where the fill is minimal, and the culvert headwalls affect the flow of traffic. However, for sidehill viaduct structures record the actual out-to-out structure width. Where the roadway is on a fill carried across a pipe or box culvert and the culvert headwalls do not affect the flow of traffic, record as blank. This is considered proper, as a filled section over a culvert simply maintains the roadway cross-section.

columns.name: Main Span Count - Item 45/B.SP.02
columns.description: The number of spans in the main or major unit.

columns.name: Main Span Material Type - Item 43A/B.SP.04
columns.description: The main spans material type.

columns.name: Main Span Design Type - Item 43B/B.SP.06
columns.description: The main spans design type.

columns.name: Approach Span Count - Item 46/B.SP.02
columns.description: The type of structure for the approach spans to a major bridge or for the spans where the structural material is different.

columns.name: Approach Span Material Type - Item 44A/B.SP.04
columns.description: The approach spans to the major bridge material type.

columns.name: Approach Span Design Type - Item 44B/B.SP.06
columns.description: The approach spans to the major bridge design type.

columns.name: Wearing Surface - Item 108A/B.SP.10
columns.description: The wearing surface and protective system of the bridge deck.

columns.name: Design Load - Item 31/B.LR.01
columns.description: The live load for which the structure was designed.

columns.name: Lanes on Structure - Item 28A/B.BH.08
columns.description: The lanes carried on the structure.

columns.name: Latitude - Item 16/B.L.05
columns.description: The latitude of the bridge.

columns.name: Longitude - Item 17/B.L.06
columns.description: The longitude of the bridge.

columns.name: Report Date
columns.description: The date the report was generated. Every row will have the same date.

columns.name: Transporation Service Center
columns.description: The MDOT Transportation Service Center (TSC)

columns.name: NHS Indicator Item 104/B.H.03
columns.description: Indicating if the structure is on the National Highway System (NHS)

columns.name: Bridge Deck Area - B.G.16
columns.description: Calculated Area of the deck by multiplying the Bridge Out-To-Out by the Bridge Length (Sft)

columns.name: Bridge Width Curb-to-Curb - Item 51/B.G.06
columns.description: The most restrictive minimum distance between curbs or rails on the structure roadway. For structures with closed medians and usually for double decked structures, recorded data will be the sum of the most restrictive minimum distances for all roadways carried by the structure. Raised or non-mountable medians, open medians, and barrier widths are to be excluded from the summation along with barrier-protected bicycle and equestrian lanes.

columns.name: LRS Route ID Item 13/B.H.06
columns.description: The identification number of the Linear Referencing Systems route the structure carries.

columns.name: LRS Mile Point Item 11/B.H.07
columns.description: The Mile Point on the Linear Referencing System where the structure exists

columns.name: Inspection Date Item 90/B.IE.02
columns.description: Date the routine bridge inspection began

columns.name: Inspection Frequency Item 91/B.IE.05
columns.description: The routine inspection interval

columns.name: Posting Status Item 41/B.IE.02
columns.description: This item provides information about the actual operational status of a structure.

columns.name: Deck Condition Rating Item 58/B.C.01
columns.description: This item describes the overall condition rating of the deck. Rate and code the condition in accordance with the above general condition ratings. Code N for culverts and other structures without decks, e.g., filled arch bridge.

columns.name: Superstructure Condition Rating Item 59/B.C.02
columns.description: This item describes the overall condition rating of the deck. Rate and code the condition in accordance with the above general condition ratings. Code N for culverts and other structures without decks, e.g., filled arch bridge.

columns.name: Substructure Condition Rating Item 60/B.C.03
columns.description: This item describes the physical condition of piers, abutments, piles, fenders, footings, or other components. Rate and code the condition in accordance with the previously described general condition ratings. Code N for all culverts.

columns.name: Culvert Condition Rating Item 62/B.C.04
columns.description: This item evaluates the alignment, settlement, joints, structural condition, scour, and other items associated with culverts. The rating code is intended to be an overall condition evaluation of the culvert. Integral wingwalls to the first construction or expansion joint shall be included in the evaluation. Item 58 - Deck, Item 59 - Superstructure, Item 59A - Paint and Item 60 - Substructure shall be coded N for all culverts.

name: Sequential Sampling - City of Flint
provider: Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE)
description: A building's plumbing system and water service line (pipes) can be made up of different types of materials. Each type of material can affect drinking water differently, so it is useful to conduct what is known as "sequential sampling". Sequential sampling is where all water usage in a building is stopped for several hours, known as "stagnation". Next, water is collected from the faucet in a series of bottles. This is done without wasting any water or running the water before filling the bottles.

name: Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome by Year
description: [About the dataset] Data includes NAS cases (numerator), all live births (denominator) and NAS rate (cases per 1,000 live birth) in Michigan, as well as by Prosperity Region, by year. [About NAS] NAS is a constellation of signs of withdrawal in neonates less than 28 days following in utero exposure to substances or prescribed medications, most commonly opioids, benzodiazepines, and barbiturates. NAS may be underreported, given increased use of non-pharmacological interventions. NAS surveillance

description: [About the dataset] Data includes NAS cases (numerator), all live births (denominator) and NAS rate (cases per 1,000 live births) by county of residence in Michigan over a 5 year period. [About NAS] NAS is a constellation of signs of withdrawal in neonates less than 28 days following in utero exposure to substances or prescribed medications, most commonly opioids, benzodiazepines, and barbiturates. NAS may be underreported, given increased use of non-pharmacological interventions. NAS surveillan

columns.name: County Boundaries
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'County Boundaries' (v2et-sdmx) the point in column 'wkt' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.
